title: Hover scale on cards
design_intent: Subtle lift indicates interactivity.
reference_inspiration: Airbnb cards
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Tiny scale (1.02-1.04).
2. Quick ease.
3. Shadow grows for depth.
solution: <motion.div whileHover={{scale: 1.03, boxShadow: '0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)'}} transition={{duration: 0.2}}>...</motion.div>
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Skeleton shimmer with CSS only
design_intent: Loading state without JS overhead.
reference_inspiration: Facebook skeleton
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Linear gradient + background-position animation.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Loading vs progress
design_intent: Use progress when possible; loop only when truly unknown.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Determinate progress always preferred.
2. Loop only as last resort.
solution:
// determinate
<progress value={done} max={total}/>
what_breaks_naive_approach: Spinner forever = anxious.

title: AnimatePresence list add/remove
design_intent: Items enter and leave gracefully.
reference_inspiration: Linear inbox
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. AnimatePresence wraps items.
2. exit animations.
solution:
<AnimatePresence initial={false}>
  {items.map(i => (
    <motion.li key={i.id}
      initial={{opacity: 0, x: -20}}
      animate={{opacity: 1, x: 0}}
      exit={{opacity: 0, x: 20}}
      layout
    >{i.label}</motion.li>
  ))}
</AnimatePresence>
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Tab bar indicator that follows selection
design_intent: Selected tab indicator slides to position.
reference_inspiration: iOS native tab indicator
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Reanimated shared value.
2. withSpring to new position.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Squash and stretch
design_intent: Drop/bounce feels weighty.
reference_inspiration: Disney
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. On impact: brief squish (scaleY 0.9, scaleX 1.1).
2. Settle to 1,1.
interaction_details: Ball drops with squish on landing
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Animation that doesn't block scroll
design_intent: Animations on scroll must not freeze scroll.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. transform/opacity only.
2. Passive listeners.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Hover-triggered tooltip
design_intent: Show tooltip after intentional hover; not flicker.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. 300ms delay before show.
2. 100ms exit.
3. AnimatePresence.

performance_strategy.interrupt_handling: Cancel show if leave before delay

what_breaks_naive_approach: Flicker on quick passes.

title: SplitText character reveal
design_intent: Reveal text character-by-character for hero impact.
reference_inspiration: Apple intros
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. SplitText plugin wraps chars.
2. gsap.from each with stagger.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Header collapse on scroll
design_intent: Header shrinks/dims as user scrolls down — more room for content.
reference_inspiration: iOS Notes
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. useScrollViewOffset.
2. Interpolate to header height.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Don't animate during user input
design_intent: User typing should never be interrupted by animation.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Defer animations to after user paused.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Janky input.

title: Number counter animation
design_intent: Counters animate to new value — show change is happening.
reference_inspiration: Dashboards
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. useMotionValue + animate.
2. interpolate or template.

accessibility.screen_reader: Final value in DOM at all times

what_breaks_naive_approach: setState per tick: re-renders too much.

title: View-timeline for in-view reveal
design_intent: Elements animate as they enter viewport.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. view-timeline + animation-range native.
solution:
.card { animation: fade-up linear; animation-timeline: view(); animation-range: entry 0% entry 100%; }
@keyframes fade-up { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(20px); } }
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Spring physics for buttons
design_intent: Natural feel via spring vs ease.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Spring: stiffness 400, damping 30 = quick + settled.
2. Higher stiffness = snappier.
solution: <motion.button whileTap={{scale: 0.96}} transition={{type: 'spring', stiffness: 400, damping: 30}}/>
what_breaks_naive_approach: Linear ease = mechanical.

title: Flatlist item animations
design_intent: List items animate in on first appearance.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Per-item enter via Layout entering.
2. Limited to visible items.
solution:
import {FadeInDown} from 'react-native-reanimated';
<Animated.View entering={FadeInDown.duration(300)}>{item}</Animated.View>
interaction_details: Each item fades in as scrolled into view
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Easing as emotional communication
design_intent: Snappy curve = energetic; settled = calm.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Match curve to emotional tone.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Reducing repaint via transform
design_intent: Animate transform, never layout.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning: The motion should communicate the state change directly, stay under a short perceptual threshold, preserve layout stability, and provide a reduced-motion fallback.
what_breaks_naive_approach: layout props.

title: Layout animations for grid sort
design_intent: Items animate to new positions when sorted.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. layout prop on items.
2. Framer measures + animates.
solution: {sorted.map(i => <motion.div key={i.id} layout>{i.label}</motion.div>)}
what_breaks_naive_approach: Snap.

{open &&
{content}
}

title: Path drawing animation
design_intent: SVG line draws progressively — guides eye.
reference_inspiration: Stripe homepage
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. pathLength=0 → 1 animates stroke-dashoffset.
solution:
<motion.path d="M0,0 L100,100" stroke="black" strokeWidth={2} fill="none"
  initial={{pathLength: 0}}
  animate={{pathLength: 1}}
  transition={{duration: 1, ease: 'easeInOut'}}
/>
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

Senior thinking:
1. @container with animation values.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Long-press scale
design_intent: Long-press scales card to indicate selection mode.
reference_inspiration: iOS
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. LongPress gesture; scale on activation.
solution:
const scale = useSharedValue(1);
const longPress = Gesture.LongPress().minDuration(500).onStart(() => { scale.value = withSpring(1.05); }).onEnd(() => { scale.value = withSpring(1); });
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: AnimatePresence with mode='popLayout'
design_intent: Smooth overlap when new element pushes old.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. popLayout removes old from layout, new takes its place.
solution:
<AnimatePresence mode="popLayout">
  <motion.div key={i} initial={{opacity: 0}} animate={{opacity: 1}} exit={{opacity: 0}}>{current}</motion.div>
</AnimatePresence>
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Motion design audit checklist
design_intent: Process for evaluating motion in product.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Inventory all animations.
2. Categorize: meaningful / decorative / accidental.
3. Remove or refine.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Drift to motion fatigue.
